Develop measurable criteria for assessing the quality of research, the reasonableness and adequacy of the basis for any recommendation or rating, and the accuracy of recommendations over time.'
rationales.C: Incorrect. The practice covers research reports, credit ratings, AND investment recommendations.

questions.stem: *V(A) compliance — scenario testing guidance* *most likely*:

options.A: Scenario-testing expectations should remain informal so analysts can adapt them freely.
options.B: Minimum testing standards apply only to models purchased from external vendors.
options.C: develop DETAILED, WRITTEN GUIDANCE that establishes MINIMUM LEVELS of scenario testing of all computer-based models used in developing, rating, and evaluating financial instruments.

rationales.A: Incorrect. The practice is DETAILED, WRITTEN guidance establishing MINIMUM scenario-testing levels.
rationales.B: Incorrect. Internally developed models are covered equally — arguably more so, given the creator's higher diligence duty.
rationales.C:
Full statement: develop DETAILED, WRITTEN GUIDANCE that establishes MINIMUM LEVELS of scenario testing of all computer-based models used in developing, rating, and evaluating financial instruments; the policy should contain criteria related to (1) breadth of scenarios tested, (2) accuracy of the output over time, and (3) analysis of CASH FLOW SENSITIVITY to inputs.

Correct. Per Standard V(A) Compliance Practices: 'Develop detailed, written guidance that establishes minimum levels of scenario testing of all computer-based models used in developing, rating, and evaluating financial instruments. The policy should contain criteria related to the breadth of the scenarios tested, the accuracy of the output over time, and the analysis of cash flow sensitivity to inputs.'

questions.stem: *V(A) compliance — outside providers* *most likely*:

options.A: develop MEASURABLE CRITERIA for assessing outside providers, including (1) quality of information being provided, (2) reasonableness and adequacy of the provider's information collection practices, and (3) accuracy of the information over time.
options.B: Outside information providers may be assessed informally through the strength and longevity of the business relationship, since years of satisfactory service demonstrate quality better than any written criteria could.
options.C: Assessment criteria for outside providers need address only the price of the service relative to competing vendors, because information quality across established commercial providers is essentially uniform.

rationales.A:
Full statement: develop MEASURABLE CRITERIA for assessing outside providers, including (1) quality of information being provided, (2) reasonableness and adequacy of the provider's information collection practices, and (3) accuracy of the information over time; the established policy should outline HOW OFTEN the provider's products are reviewed.

Correct. Per Standard V(A) Compliance Practices: 'Develop measurable criteria for assessing outside providers, including the quality of information being provided, the reasonableness and adequacy of the provider's information collection practices, and the accuracy of the information over time. The established policy should outline how often the provider's products are reviewed.'
rationales.B: Incorrect. The practice calls for MEASURABLE criteria — information quality, collection basis, and review procedures.
rationales.C: Incorrect. Quality and process criteria, not price alone, are the required dimensions.

questions.stem: *V(A) compliance — external advisers* *most likely*:

options.A: no framework.
options.B: adopt a CONSISTENT, OBJECTIVE SET OF CRITERIA for evaluating the adequacy of external advisers and subadvisers.
options.C: no framework.

rationales.A: Consistent criteria + review frequency/basis.
rationales.B:
Full statement: adopt a CONSISTENT, OBJECTIVE SET OF CRITERIA for evaluating the adequacy of external advisers and subadvisers; the policy should include HOW OFTEN and ON WHAT BASIS the allocation of funds to the external adviser or subadviser will be reviewed.

Correct. Per Standard V(A) Compliance Practices: 'Adopt a consistent, objective set of criteria for evaluating the adequacy of external advisers and subadvisers. The policy should include how often and on what basis the allocation of funds to the external adviser or subadviser will be reviewed.'
rationales.C: External adviser criteria.

questions.stem: *Rely on CFA Institute standards for adviser assessment* under V(A) *most likely*:

options.A: External advisers may not be judged by industry standards, which are self-serving marketing devices.
options.B: Adherence to recognized standards is irrelevant to adviser evaluation because standards are voluntary.
options.C: one factor in evaluating external advisers or subadvisers is whether they adhere to recognized industry standards to guide their work.

rationales.A: Incorrect. Adherence to recognized industry standards is a legitimate, recommended evaluation factor.
rationales.B: Incorrect. Voluntariness does not diminish the signal; commitment to recognized standards informs the diligence assessment.
rationales.C:
Full statement: one factor in evaluating external advisers or subadvisers is whether they adhere to recognized industry standards to guide their work; codes, standards, and guides on best practice published by CFA Institute establish practices for advisers and may be used by members and candidates as criteria for selecting external advisers or subadvisers; the following guides are available at the CFA Institute Research and Policy Center website: the CFA Institute Asset Manager Code™, the Global Investment Performance Standards (GIPS®), and the Model Request for Proposal (for equity, credit, or real estate managers).

Correct. Per Standard V(A) Guidance on Selecting External Advisers and Subadvisers: 'One factor in evaluating external advisers or subadvisers is whether they adhere to recognized industry standards to guide their work. Codes, standards, and guides on best practice published by CFA Institute establish practices for advisers and may be used by members and candidates as criteria for selecting external advisers or subadvisers. The following guides are available at the CFA Institute Research and Policy Center website: the CFA Institute Asset Manager Code™, the Global Investment Performance Standards (GIPS®), and the Model Request for Proposal (for equity, credit, or real estate managers).'

questions.stem: *Higher review for non-established sources* under V(A) *most likely*:

options.A: the sources of the information and data will INFLUENCE the level of the review a member or candidate must undertake.
options.B: no framework.
options.C: no framework.

rationales.A:
Full statement: the sources of the information and data will INFLUENCE the level of the review a member or candidate must undertake; information and data taken from certain sources, such as BLOGS, INDEPENDENT RESEARCH AGGREGATION WEBSITES, or SOCIAL MEDIA, may require a GREATER LEVEL OF REVIEW than information from more established research organizations.

Correct. Per Standard V(A) Guidance on Information Sources: 'The sources of the information and data will influence the level of the review a member or candidate must undertake. Information and data taken from certain sources, such as blogs, independent research aggregation websites, or social media, may require a greater level of review than information from more established research organizations.'
rationales.B: Higher review for less-established sources.
rationales.C: Source-based review level.

questions.stem: *Best practice for cost/service disclosure timing* under V(B) *most likely*:

options.A: Descriptions of costs and services are best provided after the engagement concludes, when actual totals are known with certainty, since estimates given at the outset risk misleading the client about final charges.
options.B: best practice is for members and candidates with client-facing responsibilities to clearly set out in WRITTEN FORM AT THE OUTSET of the engagement a description of the nature and parameters of the professional services as part of the engagement agreement, contract, or other documents stipulating the terms of the client relationship.
options.C: A thorough verbal explanation during the first client meeting is the recommended vehicle for cost and service disclosure, because conversation allows immediate questions in a way written documents cannot.

rationales.A: Incorrect. Best practice is disclosure in WRITTEN form AT THE OUTSET of the engagement, with expected ranges where totals are unknown.
rationales.B: Correct. Per Standard V(B) Guidance on Disclosing Nature of Services: 'Best practice is for members and candidates with client-facing responsibilities to clearly set out in written form at the outset of the engagement a description of the nature and parameters of the professional services as part of the engagement agreement, contract, or other documents stipulating the terms of the client relationship.'
rationales.C: Incorrect. Writing, not conversation, is the best-practice medium — it documents what the client was told.

questions.stem: *Specific dollar amounts required for costs?* under V(B) *most likely*:

options.A: yes always.
options.B: no framework.
options.C: the standard, however, does NOT require members and candidates to provide specific dollar amounts.

rationales.A: Reasonable detail, not dollars.
rationales.B: No dollar mandate.
rationales.C:
Full statement: the standard, however, does NOT require members and candidates to provide specific dollar amounts; for example, a firm that charges clients a quarterly fee based on the market value of assets under management will be unable to specify a dollar amount in advance; members and candidates should provide a REASONABLE AMOUNT OF DETAIL regarding the costs to be incurred by clients; members and candidates are also NOT required to disclose a description of the costs to the FIRM of providing those services.

Correct. Per Standard V(B) Guidance: 'Members and candidates should provide a reasonable amount of detail regarding the costs to be incurred by clients. The standard, however, does not require members and candidates to provide specific dollar amounts. For example, a firm that charges clients a quarterly fee based on the market value of assets under management will be unable to specify a dollar amount in advance. Members and candidates are also not required to disclose a description of the costs to the firm of providing those services.'

questions.stem: *Notify about external advisers* under V(B) *most likely*:

options.A: a firm's investment process may include the use of external advisers to manage various portions of clients' assets under management.
options.B: Clients may be told only about personnel employed directly by the firm, never external advisers.
options.C: Use of external advisers is a trade secret that V(B) shields from client disclosure.

rationales.A:
Full statement: a firm's investment process may include the use of external advisers to manage various portions of clients' assets under management; members and candidates must inform clients about the USE OF EXTERNAL ADVISERS as part of their investment process; this disclosure allows clients to understand the full mix of products and strategies being applied that may affect their investment objectives; disclosure of the use of external advisers also provides insight into the capabilities of the investment manager and the manager's reliance on other investment professionals to provide services.

Correct. Per Standard V(B) Guidance on Disclosures Regarding the Investment Process: 'A firm's investment process may include the use of external advisers to manage various portions of clients' assets under management. Members and candidates must inform clients about the use of external advisers as part of their investment process.'
rationales.B: Incorrect. Where the process includes external advisers managing portions of client assets, that use is part of the process clients should understand.
rationales.C: Incorrect. Far from shielding it, the standard treats external-manager use as disclosable process information.

questions.stem: *Leverage disclosure* under V(B) *most likely*:

options.A: Leverage disclosure is optional because sophisticated clients understand complex instruments implicitly.
options.B: members and candidates must adequately disclose the general market-related risks and the risks associated with the use of complex financial instruments that are deemed significant.
options.C: Only realized losses from leverage, not its prospective risks, require client communication.

rationales.A: Incorrect. Risks from leverage and complex instruments MUST be adequately disclosed regardless of presumed sophistication.
rationales.B:
Full statement: members and candidates must adequately disclose the general market-related risks and the risks associated with the use of complex financial instruments that are deemed significant; other types of risks that members and candidates may consider disclosing include but are not limited to counterparty risk, country risk, sector or industry risk, security-specific risk, and credit risk; in general, THE USE OF LEVERAGE CONSTITUTES A SIGNIFICANT RISK AND MUST BE DISCLOSED.

Correct. Per Standard V(B) Guidance on Identifying Risks and Limitations: 'In general, the use of leverage constitutes a significant risk and must be disclosed.'
rationales.C: Incorrect. The duty is prospective risk disclosure, not after-the-fact loss reporting.

questions.stem: *Liquidity and capacity disclosure* under V(B) *most likely*:

options.A: Liquidity constraints need no disclosure because all investments carry some liquidity risk.
options.B: Strategy capacity limits are competitive information that should be withheld from clients.
options.C: investment securities and vehicles may have LIMITING FACTORS that influence a client's or prospective client's investment decision.

rationales.A: Incorrect. Liquidity and similar LIMITING FACTORS can be decision-relevant and belong in communications when significant.
rationales.B: Incorrect. Capacity constraints that could influence the investment decision are disclosable, not withheld.
rationales.C:
Full statement: investment securities and vehicles may have LIMITING FACTORS that influence a client's or prospective client's investment decision; members and candidates must report to clients and prospective clients the existence of limitations significant to the decision-making process; examples of such factors and attributes include but are not limited to investment LIQUIDITY and CAPACITY; liquidity is the ability to liquidate an investment on a timely basis at a reasonable cost; capacity is the investment amount beyond which returns will be negatively affected by new investments.

Correct. Per Standard V(B) Guidance on Identifying Risks and Limitations: 'Investment securities and vehicles may have limiting factors that influence a client's or prospective client's investment decision. Members and candidates must report to clients and prospective clients the existence of limitations significant to the decision-making process. Examples of such factors and attributes include but are not limited to investment liquidity and capacity. Liquidity is the ability to liquidate an investment on a timely basis at a reasonable cost. Capacity is the investment amount beyond which returns will be negatively affected by new investments.'

questions.stem: *Methodology change disclosure* under V(B) *most likely*:

options.A: members and candidates must SUPPORT investment advice based on quantitative research and analysis with readily available reference material.
options.B: Quantitative advice needs no supporting reference material if the model is proprietary.
options.C: Support materials are needed only when a regulator examines the recommendation.

rationales.A:
Full statement: members and candidates must SUPPORT investment advice based on quantitative research and analysis with readily available reference material; members and candidates must ALSO DISCLOSE any changes in methodology.

Correct. Per Standard V(B) Guidance on Disclosing Factors Important to Investment Analyses and Recommendations: 'Members and candidates must support investment advice based on quantitative research and analysis with readily available reference material. Members and candidates must also disclose any changes in methodology.'
rationales.B: Incorrect. Advice grounded in quantitative research must be SUPPORTED by readily available reference material.
rationales.C: Incorrect. The material must be readily available as a matter of course, not assembled for examinations.

questions.stem: *Abbreviated recommendations* under V(B) *most likely*:

options.A: A recommended-stock list is self-contained and imposes no further communication duties.
options.B: if recommendations are contained in ABBREVIATED FORM (such as a recommended stock list), members and candidates should NOTIFY clients that additional information and analyses are available from the producer of the report.
options.C: Abbreviated recommendations are prohibited because they cannot convey adequate basis.

rationales.A: Incorrect. Abbreviated formats require NOTIFYING clients that additional supporting information is available on request.
rationales.B: Correct. Per Standard V(B) Guidance: 'If recommendations are contained in abbreviated form (such as a recommended stock list), members and candidates should notify clients that additional information and analyses are available from the producer of the report.'
rationales.C: Incorrect. Lists and other brief formats are permitted with the availability notice.

questions.stem: *Best practice — written disclosure* under V(B) *most likely*:

options.A: All V(B) disclosures must be delivered verbally to allow client questions in real time.
options.B: The Standards prescribe a single mandatory disclosure template for all firms.
options.C: the manner in which members and candidates provide the disclosures required by Standard V(B) is at their individual discretion so long as the disclosures are APPROPRIATE, ACCURATE, TIMELY, and COMPLETE.

rationales.A: Incorrect. The manner is at the member's discretion (written form is best practice); the touchstone is that disclosures are appropriate and complete.
rationales.B: Incorrect. No template is prescribed — adequacy and clarity, not format, are the requirements.
rationales.C:
Full statement: the manner in which members and candidates provide the disclosures required by Standard V(B) is at their individual discretion so long as the disclosures are APPROPRIATE, ACCURATE, TIMELY, and COMPLETE; best practice dictates that such disclosures be made in WRITTEN FORM.

Correct. Per Standard V(B) Guidance on Manner of Disclosures: 'The manner in which members and candidates provide the disclosures required by Standard V(B) is at their individual discretion so long as the disclosures are appropriate, accurate, timely, and complete. Best practice dictates that such disclosures be made in written form.'

questions.stem: *Firm-generated disclosures inadequate* under V(B) *most likely*:

options.A: while members and candidates rely on and can be directed to use firm-generated disclosures, members and candidates should ENSURE that these disclosures meet the requirements of the Code and Standards.
options.B: Members bear no responsibility for firm-generated disclosures they are directed to use.
options.C: If firm disclosures are inadequate, the member's only recourse is silence to preserve consistency.

rationales.A:
Full statement: while members and candidates rely on and can be directed to use firm-generated disclosures, members and candidates should ENSURE that these disclosures meet the requirements of the Code and Standards; they have the responsibility to alert their firm when the communication is lacking, flawed, or insufficient to meet their responsibilities under the Code and Standards; in such cases, members and candidates should, when possible, SUPPLEMENT disclosures from the firm that they consider insufficient to meet the requirements of this standard; ultimately, if members and candidates cannot rectify or supplement inadequate disclosures mandated by the firm, they should DOCUMENT their objections and take steps to DISSOCIATE from the activity.

Correct. Per Standard V(B) Guidance on Manner of Disclosures: 'While members and candidates rely on and can be directed to use firm-generated disclosures, members and candidates should ensure that these disclosures meet the requirements of the Code and Standards. They have the responsibility to alert their firm when the communication is lacking, flawed, or insufficient to meet their responsibilities under the Code and Standards. In such cases, members and candidates should, when possible, supplement disclosures from the firm that they consider insufficient to meet the requirements of this standard. Ultimately, if members and candidates cannot rectify or supplement inadequate disclosures mandated by the firm, they should document their objections and take steps to dissociate from the activity.'
rationales.B: Incorrect. Members should ensure firm-generated disclosures meet the standard when used with their clients.
rationales.C: Incorrect. The path is alerting the firm, supplementing where permitted, and dissociating if adequacy cannot be achieved.

questions.stem: *Selection of relevant factors — case-by-case* under V(B) *most likely*:

options.A: Reasonable judgment in factor selection is verified against a standardized industry checklist.
options.B: because the selection of relevant factors is an analytical skill, determination of whether a member or candidate has used reasonable judgment in excluding and including information in research reports depends heavily on CASE-BY-CASE REVIEWS rather than a specific checklist.
options.C: Excluding any factor from a communication is a per se violation of V(B).

rationales.A: Incorrect. Factor selection is an ANALYTICAL SKILL judged case by case, not by checklist.
rationales.B:
Full statement: because the selection of relevant factors is an analytical skill, determination of whether a member or candidate has used reasonable judgment in excluding and including information in research reports depends heavily on CASE-BY-CASE REVIEWS rather than a specific checklist; members and candidates should encourage their firms to have a rigorous methodology for reviewing research that is created for publication and dissemination to clients.

Correct. Per Standard V(B) Compliance Practices: 'Because the selection of relevant factors is an analytical skill, determination of whether a member or candidate has used reasonable judgment in excluding and including information in research reports depends heavily on case-by-case reviews rather than a specific checklist. Members and candidates should encourage their firms to have a rigorous methodology for reviewing research that is created for publication and dissemination to clients.'
rationales.C: Incorrect. Exclusion of less-important factors is expected; the question is whether judgment was reasonable.

questions.stem: *Report support records maintained* under V(B) *most likely*:

options.A: Once a report has been released and distributed, its supporting records may be discarded to reduce storage burden, since the published document itself becomes the permanent record of the analysis.
options.B: Supporting information behind a released report must be furnished only to regulators conducting examinations, and never to clients or prospects, whose access is limited to the published document.
options.C: to assist in the review of a report after its release, the member or candidate must MAINTAIN RECORDS indicating the nature of the research and should, if asked, be able to SUPPLY ADDITIONAL INFORMATION to the client (or any user of the report) covering factors NOT INCLUDED in the report.

rationales.A: Incorrect. Records indicating the nature of the research must be MAINTAINED to permit post-release review.
rationales.B: Incorrect. The member should be able to supply supporting information to clients and prospects on request.
rationales.C: Correct. Per Standard V(B) Compliance Practices: 'To assist in the review of a report after its release, the member or candidate must maintain records indicating the nature of the research and should, if asked, be able to supply additional information to the client (or any user of the report) covering factors not included in the report.'

questions.stem: *Fair delivery via electronic/digital* under V(B) *most likely*:

options.A: when providing information to clients in electronic or digital format, members and candidates must take reasonable steps to ensure that such delivery treats ALL CLIENTS FAIRLY.
options.B: Digital delivery is exempt from fair-dealing analysis because platforms distribute instantly.
options.C: Members may prioritize digital delivery to premium-tier clients as a service differentiator.

rationales.A:
Full statement: when providing information to clients in electronic or digital format, members and candidates must take reasonable steps to ensure that such delivery treats ALL CLIENTS FAIRLY; members and candidates using any social media service to communicate business information must be DILIGENT in their efforts to avoid unintended problems because these services may not be available to all clients.

Correct. Per Standard V(B) Guidance: 'When providing information to clients in electronic or digital format, members and candidates must take reasonable steps to ensure that such delivery treats all clients fairly. Members and candidates using any social media service to communicate business information must be diligent in their efforts to avoid unintended problems because these services may not be available to all clients.'
rationales.B: Incorrect. Electronic delivery still requires reasonable steps to treat ALL intended recipients fairly — platform speed does not guarantee equal access.
rationales.C: Incorrect. Tiered early access to recommendations is the unfairness the guidance targets.

questions.stem: *Supporting documentation examples* under V(C) *most likely*:

options.A: trade tickets only.
options.B: examples of supporting documentation include: (1) PERSONAL NOTES from meetings with the covered company.
options.C: no framework.

rationales.A: Eight examples.
rationales.B:
Full statement: examples of supporting documentation include: (1) PERSONAL NOTES from meetings with the covered company; (2) PRESS RELEASES or presentations issued by the covered company; (3) COMPUTER-BASED MODEL OUTPUTS AND ANALYSES; (4) COMPUTER-BASED MODEL INPUT PARAMETERS; (5) RISK ANALYSES of securities' impacts on a portfolio; (6) SELECTION CRITERIA for external advisers; (7) NOTES FROM MEETINGS WITH CLIENTS; and (8) OUTSIDE RESEARCH REPORTS.

Correct. Per Standard V(C) Guidance: 'The following are examples of supporting documentation that assists the member or candidate in meeting the requirements for record retention: personal notes from meetings with the covered company, press releases or presentations issued by the covered company, computer-based model outputs and analyses, computer-based model input parameters, risk analyses of securities' impacts on a portfolio, selection criteria for external advisers, notes from meetings with clients, and outside research reports.'
rationales.C: Documentation examples.

questions.stem: *Electronic/digital format records* under V(C) *most likely* include:

options.A: no framework.
options.B: no framework.
options.C: examples of electronic or digital formats for records that must be retained include but are not limited to.

rationales.A: Email/text/blog/social.
rationales.B: Electronic formats.
rationales.C:
Full statement: examples of electronic or digital formats for records that must be retained include but are not limited to: EMAILS, TEXT MESSAGES, BLOG POSTS, and SOCIAL MEDIA POSTS.

Correct. Per Standard V(C) Guidance: 'Examples of electronic or digital formats for records that must be retained include but are not limited to emails, text messages, blog posts, and social media posts.'

questions.stem: *Nature/format doesn't remove responsibility* under V(C) *most likely*:

options.A: electronic and online formats for gathering and sharing information create CHALLENGES in maintaining the appropriate records and files.
options.B: Information gathered through online formats is exempt from record-keeping requirements.
options.C: Chat and messaging content is inherently transient and cannot be subject to retention duties.

rationales.A:
Full statement: electronic and online formats for gathering and sharing information create CHALLENGES in maintaining the appropriate records and files; the nature or format of the information does NOT REMOVE members' and candidates' responsibility to maintain a record of information used in their analysis or communicated to clients.

Correct. Per Standard V(C) Guidance: 'Electronic and online formats for gathering and sharing information create challenges in maintaining the appropriate records and files. The nature or format of the information does not remove members' and candidates' responsibility to maintain a record of information used in their analysis or communicated to clients.'
rationales.B: Incorrect. Electronic formats CHALLENGE record maintenance; they do not excuse it.
rationales.C: Incorrect. Transient media used in professional analysis must still be captured in appropriate records.

questions.stem: *Records as firm property* under V(C) *most likely*:

options.A: Research records belong personally to the analyst who created them.
options.B: as a general matter, records created as part of members' and candidates' professional activity on behalf of their employer are the PROPERTY OF THE FIRM.
options.C: Record ownership transfers to the member automatically upon leaving the firm.

rationales.A: Incorrect. Records created in professional activity for the employer are FIRM property.
rationales.B:
Full statement: as a general matter, records created as part of members' and candidates' professional activity on behalf of their employer are the PROPERTY OF THE FIRM; when members and candidates leave a firm to seek other employment, they cannot take the property of the firm, including original forms or copies of supporting records of their work, to the new employer WITHOUT THE EXPRESS CONSENT of the previous employer.

Correct. Per Standard V(C) Guidance on Records Are Property of the Firm: 'As a general matter, records created as part of members' and candidates' professional activity on behalf of their employer are the property of the firm. When members and candidates leave a firm to seek other employment, they cannot take the property of the firm, including original forms or copies of supporting records of their work, to the new employer without the express consent of the previous employer.'
rationales.C: Incorrect. Departure changes nothing; taking records requires the firm's consent.

questions.stem: *Individual archive practice* under V(C) *most likely*:

options.A: Individuals must maintain personal duplicate archives of all firm records, since V(C) burdens them directly.
options.B: Firms have no role in record maintenance; the duty is entirely individual.
options.C: the responsibility to maintain records that support investment action generally rests with the FIRM rather than individuals.

rationales.A: Incorrect. The maintenance responsibility GENERALLY RESTS WITH THE FIRM; individual duplicate archives are not the requirement.
rationales.B: Incorrect. The general rule is the reverse — the firm carries the maintenance responsibility.
rationales.C:
Full statement: the responsibility to maintain records that support investment action generally rests with the FIRM rather than individuals; members and candidates should, however, ARCHIVE research notes and other documents, either electronically or in hard copy, that support their current investment-related communications EVEN IF IT IS NOT REQUIRED BY THE FIRM; doing so will assist their firms in complying with requirements for preservation of internal or external records.

Correct. Per Standard V(C) Compliance Practices: 'The responsibility to maintain records that support investment action generally rests with the firm rather than individuals. Members and candidates should, however, archive research notes and other documents, either electronically or in hard copy, that support their current investment-related communications even if it is not required by the firm. Doing so will assist their firms in complying with requirements for preservation of internal or external records.'

questions.stem: *Awareness of firm and regulatory rules* under V(C) *most likely*:

options.A: members and candidates must be AWARE of and understand employer policies and regulatory rules relating to record retention.
options.B: Record-retention rules concern the records department alone; members need not know them.
options.C: Awareness of retention rules matters only for members in supervisory roles.

rationales.A:
Full statement: members and candidates must be AWARE of and understand employer policies and regulatory rules relating to record retention; however, these policies and regulations may LAG BEHIND the advent of new communication methods, which places a GREATER RESPONSIBILITY on the individual for retaining all relevant records.

Correct. Per Standard V(C) Guidance on Firm and Regulatory Requirements: 'Members and candidates must be aware of and understand employer policies and regulatory rules relating to record retention. However, these policies and regulations may lag behind the advent of new communication methods, which places a greater responsibility on the individual for retaining all relevant records.'
rationales.B: Incorrect. Members and candidates must themselves be AWARE of and understand applicable retention policies and rules.
rationales.C: Incorrect. The awareness duty applies to all members whose work generates or relies on records.

questions.stem: *Fulfilling regulatory/firm requirements satisfies V(C)* *most likely*:

options.A: Local regulatory retention requirements are optional benchmarks a member may substitute with judgment.
options.B: local regulators often impose requirements on members, candidates, and their firms related to record retention time frames that members and candidates must follow.
options.C: Satisfying firm and regulatory retention rules still leaves a separate, undefined V(C) obligation.

rationales.A: Incorrect. Regulatory retention requirements MUST be followed where they apply.
rationales.B:
Full statement: local regulators often impose requirements on members, candidates, and their firms related to record retention time frames that members and candidates must follow; firms may also implement policies detailing the applicable time frame for retaining research and client communication records; FULFILLING SUCH REGULATORY AND FIRM REQUIREMENTS SATISFIES the requirements of Standard V(C).

Correct. Per Standard V(C) Guidance on Firm and Regulatory Requirements: 'Local regulators often impose requirements on members, candidates, and their firms related to record retention time frames that members and candidates must follow. Firms may also implement policies detailing the applicable time frame for retaining research and client communication records. Fulfilling such regulatory and firm requirements satisfies the requirements of Standard V(C).'
rationales.C: Incorrect. Fulfilling applicable regulatory and firm requirements SATISFIES the standard.

questions.stem: *Retention scope — role dependent* under V(C) *most likely*:

options.A: One universal set of records applies to every member regardless of function.
options.B: Only portfolio managers, not analysts, carry record-support obligations.
options.C: which records are required to support recommendations or investment actions DEPENDS ON THE ROLE of the member or candidate in the investment decision-making process.

rationales.A: Incorrect. Required records DEPEND ON THE MEMBER'S ROLE in the decision-making process.
rationales.B: Incorrect. Analysts, managers, and others each retain the records appropriate to their role.
rationales.C:
Full statement: which records are required to support recommendations or investment actions DEPENDS ON THE ROLE of the member or candidate in the investment decision-making process; a research analyst's supporting records will differ from a portfolio manager's; the retention obligation applies to the artifacts that substantiate that individual's scope of work and reasoning.

Correct. Per Standard V(C) Guidance: 'Which records are required to support recommendations or investment actions depends on the role of the member or candidate in the investment decision-making process.'
